title: Each muscle softening, one by one
subtitle: A ten-minute bedtime visualisation for releasing physical tension through an image of gradual softening.
excerpt: A ten-minute visualisation for releasing held muscle tension at bedtime. The image is simple: each area of the body softening in sequence, like wax warming, from feet to head. Use in bed.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, developed for the calm-the-body theme of Day 3 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Progressive muscle relaxation through visualisation is a public-domain technique.

content.text: A ten-minute visualisation for held muscles at bedtime. The image is gradual softening: each part of the body becoming softer in sequence, from feet to head. Do it lying down.
Lie down. Eyes closed. Three slow breaths.

Picture your feet. See them in the image as made of something slightly firm, not rigid, just held. Now see that quality softening. The muscles of the feet releasing their grip, becoming heavier, sinking a centimetre deeper into the mattress.
Move the image up to your calves. The same softening. Whatever holding is there, see it releasing, muscle by muscle.
Thighs. Hips. Lower back. The softening continues. Each area releasing in the image before it releases in the body.
Belly. Chest. The muscles around the ribs. The softening is slower here. Let it be.
Shoulders. The image shows them dropping half a centimetre. Arms, wrists, hands. The muscles across the top of the back.
Neck. The jaw. The face. The forehead. The scalp.
The whole body soft in the image. Heavy. Still. Let sleep arrive from here.

Written for homemade.education for Day 3 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive by Rebecca J Page. Picturing the muscles softening one by one is a standard relaxation method that has been used in sleep work for decades. See sourceNotes for the lineage.
